name: Trinidad and Tobago
avoid: June–November is wetter, greener, and more humid, with rougher north-coast seas and more washed-out hiking plans.
Carnival week is legendary but pushes Port of Spain room rates and the Trinidad–Tobago air bridge far above normal.
Do not leave Maracas or Blanchisseuse drives for after dark — the North Coast Road is scenic but slow and tiring at night.
stopoverNote: Most India itineraries connect via London, New York, Toronto, or Panama; keep Tobago for the end so the short Caribbean Airlines hop works as a clean finale.

cities.name: Port of Spain
cities.notes: Capital base for Savannah walks, Fort George sunsets, Carnival energy, Ariapita Avenue dining, and easy airport or water-taxi logistics.

cities.name: Tobago
cities.notes: Use Crown Point or nearby Bon Accord as your base for Pigeon Point, Buccoo Reef, Scarborough fort history, and slower east-coast beach days.

cities.name: Maracas Bay
cities.notes: Trinidad's classic north-coast beach stop for bake-and-shark, dramatic viewpoints, and a half-day escape from the capital.

cities.name: Caroni Swamp
cities.notes: Mangrove wetland famous for sunset scarlet ibis returns, quiet channels, and bird-heavy late-afternoon boat trips.

cities.name: Blanchisseuse
cities.notes: Remote-feeling north-coast village with river-mouth swims, easy waterfall add-ons, and one of Trinidad's prettiest daylight drives.

itinerary.sim: bmobile or Digicel prepaid data usually lands around ₹1,200–₹2,500 for enough maps, WhatsApp, ride coordination, and Caribbean Airlines check-ins; set it up in Port of Spain before north-coast or Tobago days.

Port of Spain.name: Port of Spain
Port of Spain.note: Stay around St. Ann's, Newtown, or Woodbrook for easier restaurant access, shorter airport transfers, and simple water-taxi or Carnival logistics.

days.theme: Savannah icons, Fort George sunset, and a first taste of the capital

activities.name: Queen's Park Savannah, Magnificent Seven, and Royal Botanic Gardens walk
Fort George lookout and gun battery
National Museum and Art Gallery
Breakfast Shed or Ariapita Avenue dinner crawl

days.theme: Water taxi views, Asa Wright birds, and a relaxed city night

activities.name: Port of Spain–San Fernando water taxi return
Asa Wright Nature Centre guided day visit
Queen's Park Oval and Stollmeyer's Castle exterior circuit
Ariapita Avenue bar or live-music stop

Maracas Bay.name: Maracas Bay
Maracas Bay.note: Best as a daylight outing from Port of Spain; leave early to beat road traffic and arrive before the busiest lunch window at the beach stalls.

days.theme: North-coast viewpoints, beach time, and the island's signature sandwich

activities.name: Maracas Bay beach time and lookout stops on the North Coast Road
Richard's Bake and Shark lunch
PTSC bus from City Gate to Maracas or shared taxi return
activities.cost: ₹87 pp by PTSC / ₹620–₹1,240 pp by taxi

activities.name: Las Cuevas beach add-on if sea conditions stay calm

Caroni Swamp.name: Caroni Swamp
Caroni Swamp.note: Plan this around the late-afternoon boat because the scarlet ibis return to roost is the main reason most travelers come here.

days.theme: Mangrove channels, scarlet ibis, and a gentle central-Trinidad evening

activities.name: Nanan's Caroni Bird Sanctuary sunset boat tour
Caroni Visitor Centre and waterside boardwalk area
Temple in the Sea or Waterloo photo stop if using a driver
Casual doubles or roti dinner on the way back

Blanchisseuse.name: Blanchisseuse
Blanchisseuse.note: Carry cash, snacks, and swimwear, and avoid rushing — this is one of Trinidad's prettiest road trips rather than a place to over-schedule.

days.theme: Scenic north-coast drive, waterfall detour, and river-mouth swims

activities.name: Blanchisseuse beach and Marianne River mouth stop
Avocat Waterfall walk with local guide tip
Paria Bay trail start or short coastal walk
Taxi or private driver from Port of Spain for the full north-coast loop

Tobago.name: Tobago
Tobago.note: Base in Crown Point or Bon Accord for the easiest flight logistics, then branch to Scarborough, Buccoo, Speyside, or Castara based on weather and energy.

days.theme: Pigeon Point, Buccoo Reef, and an easy beach-first arrival

activities.name: Caribbean Airlines domestic flight from Port of Spain to Tobago
Pigeon Point Heritage Park entry
Buccoo Reef glass-bottom boat and Nylon Pool stop
Store Bay sunset and beach-bar time

days.theme: Scarborough fort history, waterfall greenery, and the island's quieter side

activities.name: Fort King George and Tobago Museum
Argyle Waterfall guided entry
Scarborough market and harbor walk
Bacolet or Mt Irvine beach stop

days.theme: Castara or Speyside finale with birds, bays, and slow travel time

activities.name: Englishman's Bay or Castara beach day
Main Ridge Forest Reserve birding or guided easy hike
Little Tobago or Speyside boat trip
Optional extra night in Crown Point before departure

connections.method: PTSC bus from City Gate or taxi over the North Coast Road (45–60 min)
connections.cost: ₹87 pp by PTSC / ₹620–₹1,240 pp by taxi

connections.method: Taxi or private driver back across Port of Spain and central Trinidad (about 1.5–2 h)
Private driver or rental car via the Eastern Main Road and North Coast Road (2–2.5 h)
Road transfer to Piarco then Caribbean Airlines domestic flight to Crown Point (allow 4–5 h door-to-door)
connections.cost: ₹4,300–₹6,800 pp including road transfer and flight

extras: PTSC buses are the cheapest option, but beach and long-distance frequencies are limited, so confirm times at City Gate before building a no-taxi day around them.
The Port of Spain–San Fernando water taxi is a fun scenic commuter ride, but it does not replace the Trinidad–Tobago air bridge — fly or take the ferry for Tobago.
Carry cash for Maracas food stalls, north-coast lay-bys, and some small Tobago guesthouses; cards are much more reliable in bigger city hotels.
Carnival usually falls in February or early March: brilliant if intentional, exhausting if not, and one of the few periods when even domestic flights sell out early.
If red flags are up or surf looks rough on the north coast, swap swim time for lookout stops, Fort George, or a longer Port of Spain food day.

Port of Spain: Queens Park Savannah Port of Spain
